label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out the best title for this passage. The question gives four options: A, B, C, D. The passage is about Will Steger leading a team on a dogsled trip to the Arctic to show the effects of global warming. First, I'll read through the passage again to get the main idea. The author starts by saying if you want to see the effects of global warming, go north. Will Steger is taking people there. He's a famous polar explorer and environmentalist. The team is going on a long dogsled trip across Ellesmere Island in the Canadian Arctic. They're documenting it online so others can follow. The purpose is to show the front lines of global warming. The passage also mentions that climate change has already changed the Arctic's geography and that within a decade, reaching the North Pole by dog team might require flotation, meaning the ice is melting. Now, the question is about the best title. Let's look at the options again. A) The Arctic Is In Danger – This focuses on the danger to the Arctic. The passage does talk about the Arctic being affected by global warming, but the main point isn't just that it's in danger; it's about showing the effects through Steger's expedition and raising awareness. B) Discoveries of the Arctic – The passage isn't about new discoveries but about the impact of global warming. The team is going to observe and document the effects, not make new discoveries in the traditional sense. C) Sledding Through the Arctic – This is about the method of travel. The dogsled trip is part of the story, but the main purpose is to highlight global warming. The focus isn't just on the journey but on the environmental message. D) Consequences of Global Warming – This is a broad option. The passage is specifically about showing the effects of global warming through the Arctic expedition. It's about the consequences, like melting ice and changing geography, and how human actions can change the future. So, which is better? The passage's main theme is the effects of global warming as demonstrated in the Arctic. The title should capture that. While A mentions the Arctic in danger, D is more about the consequences of global warming, which is what the expedition is about. The title should reflect the purpose of the trip and the content of the passage. Option D
